{{Quote|During a battle, those Rebels shot his ship down. He's good enough, and lucky enough to have crash-landed instead of blowing up.|[[Ceth Laike]], about the TIE bomber pilot|<ref name="SWG:E">{{SWGcite|exp=JtL|type=faction|Imperial|Enough!!|Talus}}</ref>}}
A minor '''battle''' between the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] took place on [[Talus/Legends|Talus]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The battle costed the Imperials an [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]], an [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]], and one [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bomber]].<ref name="SWG:IvRB" />

When the TIE bomber was shot down by Rebels, the lucky [[TIE pilot|pilot]] who piloted the vessel was able to safely crash-land near the battlefield instead of blowing up. The pilot contacted [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial Officer]] [[Ceth Laike]] to request evacuation but he was assassinated on the spot by a group of [[Binayre Pirates|Binayre]] scalawags<ref name="SWG:E" /> looking for ship parts to salvage.<ref name="P14.1">{{SonyForumsCite|url=swg/board/message?board.id=swgpatchnotes&message.id=734|text=Update Notes: Publish 14.1 - March 24, 2005}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:DownedATAT.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The downed AT-AT, moments after collapse.]]
The Imperial vs. Rebel Battle was a point of interest in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:IvRB" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> This point of interest was added to the game with the update named "[[W:c:swg:Publish 1 Notes: 13 August 03|Publish 1]]," released on [[August 3]], 2003.<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2003">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64185&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2003}}</ref>
